answers.tl_q1: This quarter engineering focused on delivering the merchant onboarding APIs, completing Stripe payment gateway integration, building the analytics data pipeline using event sourcing, and upgrading the CI/CD pipeline to support zero-downtime deployments. We also addressed 14 carry-over bugs from Q1 and conducted a full security audit of the payment API layer.
answers.tl_q3: 1. Merchant onboarding APIs built and deployed — all endpoints documented, test coverage at 87%. 2. Stripe integration completed with webhook support for real-time payment events. 3. Analytics data pipeline built on event sourcing — processes up to 10,000 transactions per minute in load tests. 4. CI/CD pipeline upgraded with blue-green deployment support. 5. Security audit passed — 2 medium vulnerabilities found and patched within the quarter, 0 critical findings.
answers.tl_q4: Total tickets raised: 38. Resolved: 34. Open: 4 (all low priority). Critical incidents: 0. Major incidents: 2. Recurring issues: 1 related to session timeout edge case. Major incident 1: June 3 2026, production deployment failure on release v2.4.1, impact was 45 minutes of staging environment downtime affecting UAT session, root cause was misconfigured environment variable in deployment script, action taken was immediate rollback and config fix, current status resolved. Major incident 2: June 18 2026, analytics dashboard returning stale data for 2 hours, impact was incorrect metrics shown to customer during demo, root cause was Redis cache invalidation bug introduced in v2.5.0, action taken was hotfix deployed within 3 hours, current status resolved and regression test added.
answers.tl_q5: Code quality is good overall — peer review process maintained, average PR review time is 18 hours. QA has been thorough but slightly under-resourced with one QA engineer covering the full scope, leading to some test cases deferred. Release management improved with blue-green deployment but the two rollback incidents highlighted gaps in pre-release environment variable validation. Documentation is adequate for APIs but internal architecture docs are behind — this is tech debt we need to address in Q3. Team communication is strong internally. Team stability is good — no attrition, though the backend team is operating at near full capacity with limited buffer for unplanned work.
answers.tl_q6: Risk 1: Backend team capacity — High impact, owner Delivery Manager, mitigation is to evaluate adding one backend engineer for Q3 before public launch peak. Risk 2: Local PSP integration still pending final vendor certification — Medium impact, owner BA, mitigation is weekly follow-up with vendor and parallel preparation of fallback flow. Issue 1: Architecture documentation gap — Medium impact, owner Tech Lead, next step is to allocate 2 hours per week in Q3 for documentation catch-up. Dependency 1: NovaPay infrastructure team needs to provision production environment by July 15 for Q3 launch readiness — High impact, owner NovaPay CTO, action is formal written request sent June 25.
answers.tl_q7: Q3 must focus on three things. First, production launch readiness — hardening deployment pipelines, completing load testing at 3x expected traffic, and resolving all open medium-severity findings. Second, local PSP integration completion and certification — this is a blocker for full merchant coverage. Third, addressing tech debt: architecture documentation, test coverage improvement from 87% to above 92%, and resolving the session timeout recurring issue. We also need to add monitoring and alerting improvements before go-live to avoid repeating the stale data incident.'
